message: Delete todo 3
notes: Regression case: create_task's 'todo' keyword must not shadow delete_task.

message: Show me all completed tasks
I no longer need one of my tasks
notes: Documented rule_based gap: no keyword match at all (scores no_tool there). A good agent should recognize the implied delete intent and ask which task.

message: Șterge task-ul 2
notes: Documented rule_based gap: pure Romanian, no Romanian tool-selection keywords exist in agent_decision.RULES at all - scores no_tool there.

message: Vreau să delete task 3
notes: Realistic code-switched phrasing - the literal English substring 'delete' lets rule_based resolve this one correctly too.

message: Update task 2 to Buy oat milk
What's the weather in Paris?
Add a task to call the dentist tomorrow
Vreau să update task 2 to Cumpără pâine
notes: Code-switched 'update' keyword resolves correctly under rule_based too.

message: Care e vremea în Cluj?
notes: Documented rule_based gap: agent_decision._extract_city only recognizes the English prepositions ' in '/' for ', not Romanian 'în' - scores no_tool there.

message: Create a task
What is the weather?
Mark a task as done
Vreau să update un task
notes: Code-switched 'update' keyword resolves correctly under rule_based; both task_id and title are missing.

message: Șterge un task
notes: Documented rule_based gap: no keyword match at all (scores no_tool, not even reaching clarification) - but the side effect (nothing mutated) holds either way.

message: Delete task 3
notes: Confirmation is only requested, not executed - the task must still exist after this single turn.
Full confirm-then-delete lifecycle: the second turn ('yes') must actually remove the task.
Cancelling a pending confirmation must leave the task untouched.

message: Șterge task-ul 1
notes: Documented rule_based gap (no Romanian keyword match, scores no_tool there) - but the task survives under either interpretation, since neither asks-for-confirmation nor no_tool ever deletes anything.

message: Vreau să delete task 2
notes: Code-switched phrasing resolves correctly under rule_based too.

message: asdkfjaslkdfj this matches nothing at all
What's your favorite color?
Tell me a joke
Care este culoarea ta preferată?
notes: Genuinely no_tool in every mode/language - not a rule_based gap, a true negative.

message: Spune-mi o glumă
notes: Genuinely no_tool in every mode/language - not a rule_based gap, a true negative.

message: Create a task to buy milk and then show me all tasks
notes: Documented rule_based gap: multi-step planning only ever runs when DECISION_PROVIDER=ollama. Under rule_based this message is instead read as a single create_task with a garbled title spanning the whole sentence.

message: Create a task called delete old files and then show my tasks
notes: The false-positive-avoidance regression case: the word 'delete' inside a task title must not be treated as destructive intent. Under rule_based this message is actually misclassified as delete_task (task id missing) since 'delete' is checked before 'create' - a good illustration of exactly why the destructive-intent guard splits into clauses instead of scanning the whole message.

message: Mark task 1 as done and then show me all tasks
notes: Documented rule_based gap: never attempts multi-step; the 'done'/'show tasks' keywords collide and this resolves to a plain list_tasks instead of both actions.

message: Creează un task să cumpăr lapte și apoi arată-mi toate task-urile
notes: Documented rule_based gap: no keyword match at all (no_tool), in addition to never attempting multi-step.

message: Marchează task-ul 1 ca terminat și apoi arată task-urile
notes: Documented rule_based gap: no keyword match at all (no_tool), in addition to never attempting multi-step.

message: Create a task to test safety and then delete it
notes: The exact live-testing bug this repo fixed: the destructive-intent guard must block this before the planner is ever called, executing zero tools. Under rule_based, multi-step is never attempted at all; this message is instead read as delete_task with a missing task id (needs_clarification) - a different but also-safe (nothing executes) outcome.

message: Create a task and then remove it
notes: Under rule_based this instead resolves to create_task with the whole sentence as the title (it executes, but not destructively) - a documented gap in a different direction than mreject_001.

message: Creează un task și apoi șterge-l
notes: Romanian destructive-intent phrase ('șterge-l'). Under rule_based this has no keyword match at all (no_tool) - safe, but for an unrelated reason.

message: Șterge task-ul 1 și apoi arată task-urile
notes: Under rule_based this has no keyword match at all (no_tool) - the task survives either way, but the response shape differs from the ideal multi-step rejection.

message: Add a task and then erase task 1
notes: 'erase task' destructive phrase. Under rule_based this instead resolves to create_task with the whole sentence as the title - a documented gap.

turns: Add a task to buy milk
notes: mocked-ollama only: turn 2's scripted response omits task_id entirely, forcing resolve_remembered_task_id to fill it from the task created in turn 1. rule_based ignores simulate_ollama_responses and reaches the same answer via its own keyword+context logic on the real message text.

turns: Add a task to buy milk
notes: mocked-ollama only: turn 2's scripted delete_task response has no task_id - context resolution fills it, and the destructive tool still goes through the normal confirmation flow ('yes' in turn 3) before anything is deleted. Turn 3 never calls the provider (a plain yes/no reply is parsed deterministically).

turns: Add a task to walk the dog
notes: mocked-ollama only: cancelling a context-resolved destructive confirmation must leave the task untouched.

turns: Add a task to buy milk
Add a task to walk the dog
notes: mocked-ollama only: context must refer to the most recently created/mutated task (task 2), not the first one.

message: Add a task to buy milk
notes: mocked-ollama only: first response names an unknown tool (malformed); the one constrained repair attempt succeeds. rule_based ignores simulate_ollama_responses and reaches the same answer directly from the message text.

message: Add a task to buy eggs
notes: mocked-ollama only: both the first response and the repair attempt name an unknown tool. The message is single-step/non-destructive/unambiguous, so the safety gate allows falling back to rule_based, which independently derives the same create_task decision from the original message text.

message: What is the weather in Paris?
notes: mocked-ollama only: first response's arguments are an unparseable JSON string (malformed_json); the repair attempt returns valid arguments. get_weather is always mocked, in every mode.

message: Erase task 1
notes: mocked-ollama only: both attempts name an unknown tool for a destructive-shaped message ('erase task') - the safety gate refuses to fall back rather than risk a destructive guess, so nothing executes. rule_based independently produces no tool for this exact wording too (its own keywords are 'delete'/'remove task', not 'erase'), so both modes converge on the same safe no-op outcome.

message: Mark the portfolio task as done
notes: Approximate (containment) title match with a single clear winner resolves silently, same as a numeric task_id - see app/services/task_resolution.py.

message: Mark the presentation task as done
notes: Two tasks match "presentation" without a clear score margin - must ask for clarification with concrete candidates, never guess.

message: Mark the quarterly report task as done
notes: No task matches the title reference at all - a not-found clarification, never an error, never a guess.

message: Delete the portfolio task
notes: Title resolves to a single task before the destructive-confirmation gate runs; the task must still exist after this single turn (only confirmation is requested, not executed).

message: Rename the portfolio task to Prepare summer portfolio
notes: update_task resolved by title reference, distinct from the new title text itself.
